title: 007 packet metadata points to a different spec folder than the reviewed packet path
recommendation: Choose one canonical location, then align `packet_id`, `spec_folder`, `parent_id`, `specFolder`, `specId`, and all `_memory.continuity.packet_pointer` values with that location before considering 007 complete.
evidence: The reviewed 007 folder lives under `134-deep-context-gathering/007-deep-command-gate-hardening`, but `description.json` sets `packet_id` to `skilled-agent-orchestration/136-deep-command-gate-hardening`; `graph-metadata.json:3-5`, `spec.md:14`, `implementation-summary.md:13`, `plan.md:13`, `tasks.md:13`, and `checklist.md:13` repeat the 136 pointer. `git ls-files` also shows tracked files under `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/136-deep-command-gate-hardening` while the reviewed 134/007 folder is untracked in the current tree.
claim: 007 packet metadata points to a different spec folder than the reviewed packet path
scopeProof: Bounded to the requested 007 spec folder and git path metadata needed to verify whether the path mismatch was real.

title: 006 completion docs overclaim default-pool stale-reference cleanup
recommendation: Update the stale feature-catalog and manual-testing-playbook default-pool references to 2 native seats, or narrow the 006 completion claim to the exact surfaces that were actually cleaned.
evidence: The implementation summary marks `Het list only under Custom/example` as PASS and says no `default = het` text remains, but deep-context docs still state the opposite: `.opencode/skills/deep-context/feature_catalog/01--frontier-seeding/config-shape-and-default-pool.md:47` says the default heterogeneous pool is used, `.opencode/skills/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/01--frontier-seeding/config-shape-and-default-pool.md:14` and `:28-30` require `2 native + MiMo + gpt + deepseek`, and `.opencode/skills/deep-context/manual_testing_playbook/manual_testing_playbook.md:197-204` still describes the default heterogeneous executor pool.
claim: 006 completion docs overclaim default-pool stale-reference cleanup
scopeProof: Checked the 006 spec docs, the actual default config, and targeted deep-context documentation containing explicit default-pool references.

title: Completed packets still carry stale pre-completion next actions
recommendation: Refresh stale `_memory.continuity.next_safe_action` fields to post-completion follow-ups, or remove pre-completion actions from non-canonical continuity surfaces so resume output does not contradict complete status.
evidence: The 005 spec is `Status: Complete` and `implementation-summary.md:115` says `validate.sh --strict` passed, but frontmatter still says `next_safe_action: Run validate.sh --strict and reconcile completion metadata`. The same stale pattern appears in 006 `spec.md:18` and 007 `spec.md:18`, while their description/graph metadata mark `status` complete.
claim: Completed packets still carry stale pre-completion next actions
scopeProof: Bounded to completion/status fields in the requested spec docs and their implementation-summary verification claims.
